source: IRS Publication 550, 'Wash Sales'; IRC 1091, 1091(d), 1223(3); Treas. Reg. 1.1091-1(c). Money figures in cases 'pub550_partial' and 'full_disallowance' are legally pinned by the IRS worked example; 'multi_lot_apportionment' is a derived application of the same statute exercising per-lot cumulative rounding.

cases.description: IRS Pub 550 partial wash sale: bought 100 sh for $1,000, sold for $750 ($250 loss), bought 50 sh within 30 days for $800. Loss on 50 matched shares ($125) disallowed; $125 deductible; replacement basis $800 -> $925.

Full disallowance: 100 sh sold at $400 loss, 120 replacement sh bought within window. matched=100, entire $400 loss disallowed, none deductible, full loss added to replacement basis, holding period tacked.

Multi-lot apportionment in acquisition order with cent-level rounding: 100 sh sold at $333.33 loss; two replacement lots supplied OUT of acquisition order (later lot first). matched=70, disallowed=$233.33 apportioned $100.00 to the earlier 30-sh lot and $133.33 to the later 40-sh lot (sums exactly to disallowed).
